title: RepairBot Interface
desc: The physical and conversational UI layer of the RepairBot system — where material touch meets digital guidance. Designed to be held, consulted, and trusted during the repair process.

title: Startup Product Alpha
desc: An early-stage product for a London-based startup. Exploring how designed objects can carry emotional intelligence within everyday use.

title: Tangible Interaction Device
desc: A probe object for exploring how human hands communicate intent. Built to test hypotheses about pre-linguistic gesture in HCI.

title: Wearable Sensor Prototype
desc: Garment-integrated sensing for affective computing research. Where fashion, electronics, and body meet.

title: Repair Workshop Kit
desc: A designed toolkit for visible mending workshops — objects that teach through being used.

title: RepairBot Framework — CHI 2026
desc: A framework for human-touch-aware conversational agents in textile repair. Introduces the concept of material-sensitive dialogue and the Ideation Gap.

title: Affective Tools for Thought
desc: Workshop paper at CHI 2026. Argues that emotional engagement is not a distraction from thinking — it is its constitutive substrate.

title: Material-in-the-Loop
desc: A theoretical framework positioning physical material as an active participant in AI-assisted creative processes. Drawing on Ingold's anthropology of making.

title: Embodied Interaction & Mending
desc: Study 3 of the PhD thesis. Addresses the Ideation Gap — helping novices move from procedural skill to creative design thinking in textile repair.

title: Touch as Language in HCI
desc: A literature synthesis on how pre-linguistic, tactile knowing is excluded from computational interfaces — and what happens when we bring it back.

title: Tennis Ball Machine Mk.1
desc: A self-commissioned engineering project. A motor that spun wrong, a frame that vibrated itself apart — the slow realisation that building teaches you what you didn't know you didn't know.

title: Tennis Ball Machine Mk.2
desc: The second version. Fewer mistakes. Better frame. An ongoing documentary — honest, detailed, occasionally funny.

title: Sensor Array for Garments
desc: Custom-built capacitive sensing layer for garment research. Soldered by hand, tested repeatedly, broken twice, and finally made to work reliably.

title: Making Log: Vol. 1
desc: The first instalment of an ongoing documentary. A record of mistakes, pivots, material surprises, and small victories. A love letter to process.

title: Interaction Design Lectures
desc: A series of lectures on interaction design at UCL, exploring HCI through the lens of body, material, and cognition.

title: MSc Design Mentoring 2024
desc: Individual mentoring for MSc students navigating their first serious design research projects. Focus on building a thinking voice, not just a visual one.

title: Portfolio Agency: Cohort
desc: Mentoring students preparing portfolios for top design programmes. Worked on concept clarity, narrative structure, and the confidence to have a point of view.

title: Workshop: Embodied Making
desc: A practical workshop combining craft techniques with design research methods. Make something broken, then redesign it from the inside out.

title: Design Research Methods
desc: Teaching Research through Design as a legitimate epistemology — not just a methodology, but a way of knowing. For students who think with their hands.

title: ABQ — Creativity Series
desc: A podcast about creativity, making, and the gaps between disciplines. A space for conversations that don't fit anywhere else — with designers, researchers, engineers, and makers.

title: ABQ: Making & Thinking
desc: An episode on the relationship between making things with your hands and generating ideas in your head.

title: ABQ: Designers in Research
desc: What happens when designers enter academia? Identity, rigor, and what gets lost — and found — in translation.

title: ABQ: The Joy of Getting Stuck
desc: A celebration of creative impasse. Why being stuck is not the enemy of making — it is, often, the point.
